intro: Converting a codebase from JavaScript to TypeScript adds a robust static type layer over your existing execution infrastructure without altering the underlying runtime engine mechanics. This migration path provides developers with enhanced IDE autocompletion, compile-time error detection, and explicit documentation of internal code contracts. The transition turns implicit architecture assumptions into clear, verifiable type blueprints.

diffs.from: Dynamic types evaluated purely at runtime, permitting arbitrary property additions and mutations.
diffs.to: Static typing checked during compilation, supporting explicit interface models, generic constraints, and union types.

diffs.from: Standard ECMAScript scripting layout containing variables, structures, and function definitions.
diffs.to: Superset layout adding explicit type keywords, enums, type definitions, and access modifier declarations.

diffs.from: Managed execution heap tracked via background engine garbage collection routines.
diffs.to: Identical runtime behavior since TypeScript transpiles directly into standard JavaScript before execution.

diffs.from: Single-threaded event loop queuing asynchronous operations via Promise blocks.
diffs.to: Identical asynchronous architecture utilizing type-safe Promise declarations and typed async/await wrappers.

diffs.from: Standard browser/Node runtime tools governing variables, arrays, and IO systems.
diffs.to: Identical runtime capabilities supplemented by extensive type declaration mappings for all core APIs.

examples.title: Interface Structuring
examples.description: Transforming a loose payload into a type-safe interface declaration.

examples.title: Typed Function Arguments
examples.description: Enforcing data constraints on function parameters using inline type declarations.

tips.title: Enable Strict Flags
tips.body: Activate the 'strict: true' rule in your tsconfig.json file to enforce robust type-checking protections.

tips.title: Avoid Any Typing
tips.body: Do not bypass compiler safety with the 'any' keyword. Utilize 'unknown' for unpredictable dynamic inputs instead.

tips.title: Migrate Incrementally
tips.body: Convert your application smoothly file-by-file by updating file extensions from .js to .ts one module at a time.

tips.title: Install Type Declarations
tips.body: Download missing library typing files from the @types npm scope to ensure type safety across external third-party dependencies.

faqs.question: Does TypeScript run directly in the browser?
faqs.answer: No. TypeScript must pass through a build step to be compiled into standard JavaScript files before execution in a browser or engine environment.

faqs.question: What happens to types at runtime?
faqs.answer: Types undergo erasure during the compilation step, meaning they leave no runtime footprint or performance overhead in the final JavaScript file.

faqs.question: Are classes different in TypeScript?
faqs.answer: TypeScript classes follow the ECMAScript standard but add useful features like access modifiers (public/private) and explicit class interface implementations.
